summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on regulation, product + fintech innovation.

articles.title: FTA sues to block Tennessee law
articles.description: The fintech trade group asserts that the law is unconstitutional and will illegally impose higher costs on consumers.

articles.title: How a Nuvei-Payoneer merger would build business
articles.description: Nuvei’s hand could be strengthened in processing stablecoin transactions and reaching small and mid-sized merchants if a reported deal happens.

articles.title: Pagaya sues Klarna over alleged trade secret misappropriation
articles.description: The BNPL firm’s objective was to “absorb Pagaya’s trade secrets, use them to build its own competing capabilities, and cut Pagaya out,” the underwriting fintech alleged.

articles.title: 4 reasons the Visa-Mastercard pact survived
articles.description: Here’s the thinking of a federal judge who approved the settlement covering merchants who sued Visa and Mastercard over interchange fees.

articles.title: Bolt loses leaders
articles.description: Two top executives have exited the checkout company in recent weeks as the business reshapes its strategy with AI.

articles.title: Court approves Visa-Mastercard settlement
articles.description: A federal judge ruled Tuesday that a settlement between the card networks and 12 million merchants over card-swipe fees is fair and reasonable.

articles.title: Klarna dives deeper into banking
articles.description: The buy now, pay later provider is debuting a savings account to lure more people away from banks and into its ecosystem.

articles.title: Adyen taps North America chief
articles.description: The digital payments player appointed a new president to oversee operations in North America, where the Dutch company has been taking on U.S. rivals.
